author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Pali Canon - Vasala Sutta 1982
content:
The Vasala Sutta Seminar

Contents

History of the Vasala Sutta
Introduction, setting the scene
The beginning of the teaching
The importance of understanding the meaning of a word
The evil angry man v.116
Know him as outcaste too v.117
The noted brigand who v.118
v.118 continued. Vegetarianism
The man who takes unbid v.119
Who debt incurs, and pressed v.120
Who, coveting some grand v.121
Know him as outcaste too v.122
And he who's seen about v.123
Who, being rich, supports v.124
And he who parents strikes v.125
Who, asked about the goal v.126
Who doing evil deeds v.127
Who goes to other's house v.128
Who with false words deceives v.129
Who Brahmin or recluse v.130
Know him as outcaste too v.131
= And who exalts himself v.132
The mean and quarrelsome v.133
He who reviles the wake v.134
Who is no man of worth v.135
No outcaste is by birth v.136
Then know it too by this v.137
Bhante reads the last five verses v.138 - 142
Bhante reads the final prose
End of seminar.

Summary In the Vasala Sutta the Buddha takes a Brahmin to task for using the word 'Vasala' without having clear and definite idea of its meaning. In doing so he illustrates the precepts. This was a short weekend seminar and Bhante sticks closely to the verses of the Sutta.
